persona.name: Yassine Benali

occupation.title: Motion Graphics Designer
occupation.organization: Freelance, primarily contracted by Publicis Lyon and local startups
occupation.description: Creates animated content for advertising campaigns, social media, and brand identity projects. Works from a coworking space in the Confluence district three days a week and from home the rest. Juggles 2-3 client projects simultaneously, with deadlines that make his schedule unpredictable.

persona.education: BTS in Graphic Design from Lycee La Martiniere Diderot in Lyon (2018). Self-taught in After Effects and Cinema 4D through online courses. Considered art school in Paris but stayed in Lyon for cost of living and quality of life.
long_term_goals: Build a motion design studio with 2-3 employees within five years
Move into a larger apartment with his partner near Parc de la Tete d'Or
Develop a personal animation short film for festival submission
Visit his grandparents' hometown in Tlemcen, Algeria, which he has not seen since childhood
persona.style: Yassine is energetic, expressive, and quick-witted. He talks with his hands, uses slang naturally, and shifts between French and verlan effortlessly in casual conversation. He dresses in streetwear — oversized jackets, graphic tees, Nike Dunks. He is genuinely enthusiastic about things he likes and not shy about dismissing things he doesn't. First to suggest trying a new restaurant or service.

traits: Spontaneous and open to new experiences — early adopter by nature
Creative problem-solver who gets bored with routine
Socially confident and talkative, the connector in friend groups
Can be disorganized with admin tasks — procrastinates on invoicing
Generous with his time and recommendations
Occasionally impulsive with spending on food and gadgets
Emotionally open for a man in his circle — talks about feelings

interests: Street food and global cuisines — especially North African, Japanese, Mexican
Food delivery apps and new food tech services
likes: Trying new meal delivery services and food boxes
The convenience of having ingredients pre-portioned
Discovering recipes from cuisines he wouldn't normally attempt
Lyon's food scene — bouchons, halles, food trucks
Late-night creative work sessions with good music
Sharing food discoveries on Instagram stories
Sunday couscous at his parents' house
dislikes: Bland, uninspired food — hates the 'basic chicken and rice' aesthetic
Long-term commitments to subscriptions he might forget to use
Poor mobile app experiences — if the UX is bad, he's out
Condescending marketing that assumes he can't cook
Rigid meal plans with no customization
Waiting more than 3 days for delivery

beliefs: Food is culture — what you eat says who you are and where you come from
Technology should remove friction from daily life, not add new obligations
Supporting local and independent businesses matters, even when big platforms are more convenient
You don't need to be rich to eat well — creativity matters more than budget
skills: Motion graphics and video editing (After Effects, Premiere, Cinema 4D)
Confident home cook for North African dishes learned from his mother
Social media content creation and visual storytelling
Basic business management (invoicing, client relations — improving)

general: Discovers new products through Instagram, YouTube, and friend recommendations
Makes purchasing decisions quickly based on visual appeal and peer buzz
Cooks 3-4 times per week but also orders delivery 2-3 times
Grocery shopping is improvisational — buys what looks good at the market or Picard

morning: Wakes around 8:30-9:00 depending on the previous night's deadlines
Strong coffee with milk, scrolls Instagram and Behance for inspiration
Croissant or msemen from the bakery downstairs if feeling motivated
workday: Arrives at coworking space around 10:00 on office days
Deep creative work until 13:00, lunch at a nearby kebab or bouchon
Client calls and revisions in the afternoon
Often works until 20:00 when deadlines are tight
evening: Cooks dinner with his partner or they order via Uber Eats
Gaming session or music production for an hour
Catches up on design tutorials or YouTube essays before bed
weekend: Saturday: brunch with friends, exploring Lyon's food markets or Croix-Rousse
Sunday: couscous lunch at parents' house in Villeurbanne, afternoon nap, evening meal prep (when motivated)
Occasional photography walks along the Saone

health.physical: Good overall health. Doesn't exercise regularly but walks a lot and plays football with friends biweekly. Eats well but inconsistently — feast or skip. Slight seasonal allergies in spring.
health.mental: Generally upbeat but experiences stress spikes around freelance income uncertainty. Has considered therapy but hasn't started. Uses creative work as an emotional outlet.

relationships.name: Salma Haddad
relationships.description: Pharmacist at a pharmacy near Part-Dieu station. More structured and organized than Yassine — she handles the household admin. They bond over food and travel planning. She is Tunisian-French.

relationships.name: Fatima Benali
relationships.description: Retired seamstress who lives in Villeurbanne with Yassine's father. The family's anchor and the source of Yassine's love for cooking. Sunday couscous at her house is non-negotiable.

relationships.name: Karim Oussekine
relationships.description: Works in logistics at Amazon's Lyon warehouse. They play FIFA together online most weeks and share a fantasy football league. Karim is more skeptical about new trends — serves as Yassine's reality check.
